body_txt: A Stock Phrase originating from Westerns that illustrates how two Arch-Enemies are unable to peacefully coexist in the same place or situation, and how one of them must inevitably give way to the other—whether that's being forced to concede, leaving the town, or having a Duel to the Death . This phrase is usually said during the challenge to a duel, or in the opening banter in a Showdown at High Noon . Its first known use in a Western was in 1929, when Walter Huston 's character in The Virginian (best known for having Gary Cooper 's breakout role) says "This world isn't big enough for the both of us!" The first known variation to use the specific phrase "This town ain't big enough for the both of us" was the 1932 film The Western Code . It became commonplace in Westerns of the 1950s and 60s thanks to films like The Gunfighter , and from there became a cliche. It's been a Dead Horse Trope for decades, and is almost exclusively found in parodies and comedies (most of which are outside the Western genre) rather than being Played Straight at all. Compare Unstoppable Force Meets Immovable Object for a similarly used Stock Phrase . For an expression that is more eastern than Western, compare This Is Unforgivable! which similarly declares a refusal to coexist. Not to Be Confused with There Is Only One Bed (when there's no separate beds for two people) and Cold Equation (when there's not enough space for both people in the lifeboat or escape pod).
